text.title: Cap turns, catch the exception, summarize partial trace
text.summary: Bound every agent run with an explicit max_turns and convert the timeout into a structured fallback instead of a 500.

section.title: The cap-and-catch rule
section.p: Tool-calling agents do not detect their own non-progress: from the model's view, every tool result is forward motion. Only an external counter stops a stuck loop deterministically.

rule.statement: Set max_turns explicitly on every production agent invocation (5-10 retrieval, 15-20 coding) and wrap the call in a try/except that handles MaxTurnsExceeded by summarizing the partial trajectory with a cheap model and returning a graceful reply.
rule.rationale: Tool loops fail by silent budget burn — the LLM treats each tool result as progress regardless of whether the goal is closer. A hard cap is the only deterministic stop. Catching the exception (instead of letting it bubble) preserves the partial trace and turns a 500 into a usable answer.

example.note: Cap is explicit, exception is caught, partial trace feeds a 1-turn recovery summarizer.

example.code: res = await Runner.run(agent, user_msg) # default max_turns, uncaught return res.final_output
example.note: Default cap fires without warning, exception bubbles to a 500, partial trajectory is lost.

antipattern.description: Raising max_turns to 100+ to "stop hitting the cap".
antipattern.reason: Hides the underlying loop bug (bad tool, missing termination signal) and multiplies wasted spend. Fix the loop, do not extend the leash.

antipattern.description: Catching MaxTurnsExceeded and silently returning empty string.
antipattern.reason: User sees a blank reply, ops loses the trace. Always emit either a recovery summary or a typed error with the partial trajectory id.

section.reference: Reference handler that summarizes the partial trajectory with a cheap model.
OpenAI Agents SDK — Running Agents (max_turns docs).
openai-agents-python issue #844 — default max_turns footgun discussion.

list.item: Retrieval / Q&A agent: 5-10 turns. One search + one read + answer is typical 3 turns; 10 covers retries.
Code-editing agent (Read+Grep+Edit+Bash): 15-20 turns. Larger because tests-fix cycles can take 6-8 iterations alone.
Multi-agent supervisor: cap each subagent independently, plus a global cap on the supervisor (≤2× sum of subagent caps).
Long-horizon coding sandboxes: do NOT use max_turns; use checkpointing + human approval gates instead.
